[2018-12-10 20:52] mrguy888: I think it is inaccurate to say that the rules don't apply. The rules "apply" they were just broken consciously because they add something to the game without running into the problems that the rules were made to help avoid.
[2018-12-10 20:54] Deleted User (Deleted User): Sometimes tarsier > steelsplitter. Sometimes steelsplitter > tarsier. This doesn't break SDR... tarsier > nothing and steelsplitter > nothing is the only thing necessary. Golden armor and prompt don't break SDR.
[2018-12-10 20:54] apooche: The R stands for Rules
[2018-12-10 20:55] apooche: There are multiple rules
[2018-12-10 20:55] mrguy888: SDR has nothing to do with comparing steelsplitter and tarsier
[2018-12-10 20:55] apooche: One for example states that having more hp>less hp
[2018-12-10 20:55] apooche: More lifespan is strictly good
[2018-12-10 20:56] awaclus: >Units with improved statuses should be better than units with worse ones (e.g. units with higher hp should be better than identical units with lower hp; units with higher remaining lifespan should be better than identical units with lower remaining lifespan, etc..)
[2018-12-10 20:56] awaclus: Prompt sure sounds like an improved status
[2018-12-10 20:57] mrguy888: prompt is always better than non prompt except for examples of golden armor
[2018-12-10 20:57] mrguy888: you can't double count golden armor though
[2018-12-10 20:57] awaclus: >mfw I had to stop for a second to think whether Prompt is written in all caps or not :NotLikeThis:
[2018-12-10 20:57] mrguy888: lifespan is golden armor related
[2018-12-10 20:58] apooche: Not really... under construction would still not adhere to SDR if golden armor were removed
[2018-12-10 20:58] apooche: Because of lifespan, exhaust
[2018-12-10 20:58] mrguy888: well under constuction is just exhaust
[2018-12-10 20:59] apooche: And exhaust doesn’t adhere to SDR
[2018-12-10 20:59] Deleted User (Deleted User): The point of SDR is to avoid deliberately having less money or doing less damage or getting your units killed ever being a good thing. Prompt/invuln/exhaust doesn't cause any of these things, it doesn't break SDR.
[2018-12-10 21:00] mrguy888: but saying prompt doesn't follow sdr because prompt units can't have the sdr breaking effects is silly. the sdr breaking effects are just the sdr breaking effect
[2018-12-10 21:00] awaclus: invuln/exhaust causes you to deliberately have less units
[2018-12-10 21:00] mrguy888: "Prompt" isn't a real status. It is just the absence of the build time status.
[2018-12-10 21:01] Deleted User (Deleted User): no, invuln/exhaust cause you to want to wait a turn to convert your gold into units, that doesn't violate sdr
[2018-12-10 21:01] Deleted User (Deleted User): sdr says you would rather have more gold than less gold for free, or more units than less units for free
[2018-12-10 21:01] Deleted User (Deleted User): it says nothing about when you change gold into units
[2018-12-10 21:01] awaclus: >it says nothing about when you change gold into units
because of inflation, earlier > later
[2018-12-10 21:01] mrguy888: the people who made sdr say that golden armor breaks it.
[2018-12-10 21:01] mrguy888: If someone doesn't understand sdr it is not the people who invented it
[2018-12-10 21:02] Deleted User (Deleted User): argument by authority?
[2018-12-10 21:03] mrguy888: Umm if I say I like apples and you tell me I don't like apples then yes, I will use argument by authority.
[2018-12-10 21:03] namington: argument by "these guys literally made up the definition of the term youre using"
[2018-12-10 21:03] namington: you can argue that you think the definition is, for some reason, imperfect
[2018-12-10 21:03] namington: and should be changed to not apply in this case
[2018-12-10 21:03] Deleted User (Deleted User): they made up the definition, but the implications follow from the definition
[2018-12-10 21:03] namington: but then phrase it as "I dont think saying this fits under SDR is productive"
[2018-12-10 21:03] namington: instead of phrasing it as "this doesnt fit the definition"
[2018-12-10 21:04] namington: considering it was liteally used as an example in the definition
[2018-12-10 21:04] namington: basically, there's a fairly established meaning to the term SDR, and _as it is currently used_, it applies to this case
[2018-12-10 21:04] namington: again, you could argue that maybe itd be better to redefine it
[2018-12-10 21:05] namington: but then youre arguing normatively
[2018-12-10 21:05] Deleted User (Deleted User): they gave a definition, than an example that contradicted the definition
[2018-12-10 21:05] awaclus: argument from authority is only really a fallacy when the authority is not a reliable source of information
[2018-12-10 21:05] awaclus: in this case, they are
[2018-12-10 21:06] awaclus: (at least for this particular topic)
[2018-12-10 21:06] mrguy888: In this case, the authority is the sole source of this information.
[2018-12-10 21:06] Deleted User (Deleted User): i didn't call it a fallacy. if the only counter-argument to my statements is argument by authority then i've said my piece
[2018-12-10 21:06] awaclus: >if the only counter-argument to my statements is argument by authority then i've said my piece
that kind of implies that you think that it is a fallacy
[2018-12-10 21:06] namington: these stupid sdr arguments are what i was referencing in #unit_and_game_design earlier
[2018-12-10 21:06] namington: glad to have them back
[2018-12-10 21:07] apooche: As an example of how an SDR break could be removed, consider if Centrifuge let you click to sacrifice to get the resources instead of generating them automatically (and remove golden armor). If this were the case, then having less buildtime would be strictly good on centrifuge. However, since it isn’t the case, sometimes you would prefer to have more buildtime, to avoid wasting the red/blue. It is in this sense that it is seen as violating SDR. You could similarly redesign a bunch of units if you wanted to in such a fashion to strictly follow it everywhere.
[2018-12-10 21:07] awaclus: because if it is a valid argument (which it is), why are you complaining about it

[2018-12-10 21:10] Deleted User (Deleted User): i wonder if antima likewise breaks SDR
[2018-12-10 21:10] namington: the argument is that this is the commonly-accepted definition
[2018-12-10 21:11] namington: so if you want to go against/redefine that definition, go ahead
[2018-12-10 21:11] namington: but dont claim that it fits under the currnet one
[2018-12-10 21:11] Deleted User (Deleted User): no, the argument is that this is the commonly-accepted implication of the commonly-accepted definition
[2018-12-10 21:11] Deleted User (Deleted User): however, the commonly-accepted definition does not lead to this implication
[2018-12-10 21:11] namington: its the interpretation the community agrees on
[2018-12-10 21:11] mrguy888: build time breaks sdr. everything that sacrifices itself automatically after build time breaks sdr
[2018-12-10 21:12] Deleted User (Deleted User): the assertion was that i had the definition wrong, not the interpretation
[2018-12-10 21:12] awaclus: >i wonder if antima likewise breaks SDR
well, for the same reason, any unit that pays any cost automatically (whether it's saccing itself, exhausting itself, or otherwise) does
[2018-12-10 21:12] awaclus: the exhaust on Immolite doesn't break SDR as far as I can tell
[2018-12-10 21:12] namington: does 0 cost "as an addiional cost to construct this unit, sacrifice this unit" break sdr
[2018-12-10 21:13] awaclus: but on Iso Kronus it does
[2018-12-10 21:13] mrguy888: If iso was a click unit then it wouldn't
[2018-12-10 21:13] Deleted User (Deleted User): "
    It should be better to own more units.
    It should be better to own more resources.
    It should be better to kill the opponent’s units than to leave them alive.
    Units with improved statuses should be better than units with worse ones (e.g. units with higher hp should be better than identical units with lower hp; units with higher remaining lifespan should be better than identical units with lower remaining lifespan, etc..)"
[2018-12-10 21:14] namington: those are the common cases that SDR extends to, yes
[2018-12-10 21:14] namington: its not an exhaustive list
[2018-12-10 21:14] namington: I mean, SDR is more of a guideline anyway
[2018-12-10 21:14] namington: im not sure its woth nitpicking the fine details
[2018-12-10 21:14] namington: the use of the word "rule" is quite strong
[2018-12-10 21:14] namington: there are some clear "no-go" areas
[2018-12-10 21:15] namington: like mind control or whatever
[2018-12-10 21:15] camzace: i totally agree namington
[2018-12-10 21:15] namington: but for edge cases its just
[2018-12-10 21:15] camzace: sdr discussions are probably the worst thing this discord does regularly
[2018-12-10 21:15] camzace: fatal combination of uninteresting and pointless
[2018-12-10 21:15] awaclus: SDR discussions are super interesting
[2018-12-10 21:15] awaclus: we wouldn't do them regularly otherwise
[2018-12-10 21:16] mrguy888: If a unit breaks SDR it should raise a major red flag that makes you look at all the edge cases of the unit to make sure it doesn't break gameplay. It shouldn't instantly kill the design
[2018-12-10 21:16] camzace: hard disagree with 'super interesting'
[2018-12-10 21:16] namington: it also means you have to consider whether the unit will be a design problem in the future
[2018-12-10 21:17] namington: like "if we print a unit that does this, all of a sudden, this minor SDR break becomes really dumb" or whatever
[2018-12-10 21:17] namington: mind, the rotating sets idea elyot floats around solves that a bit
[2018-12-10 21:17] Deleted User (Deleted User): what happened to raids?
[2018-12-10 21:17] awaclus: oh and a ton of Events have SDR breaking mechanisms and they're still fun
[2018-12-10 21:17] mrguy888: they didn't get made and will not be made
[2018-12-10 21:18] mrguy888: that is what event is for

[2018-12-10 21:18] mrguy888: to do stuff you shouldn't do in the main game but are still fun

[2018-12-10 21:18] mrguy888: There have been no plans to make raids for something like 3 years
